title: Rasmancha
tagline: Rasmancha — heritage city of West Bengal

overview.short: The Rasmancha (Bengali: রাসমঞ্চ; Raasmoncho) is a historical temple located at Bishnupur, Bankura district, West Bengal, India.
overview.description: The Rasmancha (Bengali: রাসমঞ্চ; Raasmoncho) is a historical temple located at Bishnupur, Bankura district, West Bengal, India.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Shyam Ray Temple
topPlaces.description: Shyam Ray Temple (Śyāma rāẏa mandira) also known as Pancha-ratna temple, is a Krishna temple in Bishnupur town of Bankura district ,Medinipur division in Indian state of West Bengal. In this temple, the Hindu God Krishna is worshiped in the form of Shyam; along with Shyam, the murti of Radhika (Radha) is also worshipped.

topPlaces.name: Nikhil Banga Sikhsan Mahavidyalaya
topPlaces.description: Nikhil Banga Sikhsan Mahavidyalaya is an institution of higher education situated at Bishnupur, Bankura district, in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Jor Bangla Temple
topPlaces.description: Keshto Ray Temple, also known as Jor-Bangla Temple, is a Krishna temple at Bishnupur city of Medinipur Division. In the past, the Hindu god Krishna was worshiped as Keshto Ray in this temple.

topPlaces.name: Mrinmoyee Temple
topPlaces.description: Mrinmoyee Temple is an ancient temple in the town of Bishnupur in Bankura district of the Indian state of West Bengal . It was founded in the 10th century by the Malla king Jagat Malla.

topPlaces.name: Radha Shyam Temple
topPlaces.description: Radha Shyam Temple, also known as Rādhāśyāma Mandir, is a Krishna temple in Bishnupur town of Medinipur division in the Indian state of West Bengal. In this temple, the Hindu god Krishna is worshiped in the form of Shyam; along with Shyam, the murti of Radha is also worshipped.

topPlaces.name: Lalji Temple
topPlaces.description: Lalji Temple, Located in Bishnupur in Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Jor Mandir
topPlaces.description: Jor Mandir also known as group of temples, Located in Bishnupur in Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Temples of Bishnupur
topPlaces.description: The Temples of Bishnupur are a group of temples located in the town of Bishnupur, West Bengal, India. These temples, built by the Malla kings between the 17th and 18th centuries, are renowned for their unique architectural style that blends traditional Bengali design with influences from other regions.

items.note: Shyam Ray Temple (Śyāma rāẏa mandira) also known as Pancha-ratna temple, is a Krishna temple in Bishnupur town of Bankura district ,Medinipur division in Indian state of West Bengal. In this temple, the Hindu God Krishna is worshiped in the form of Shyam; along with Shyam, the murti of Radhika (Radha) is also worshipped.

Keshto Ray Temple, also known as Jor-Bangla Temple, is a Krishna temple at Bishnupur city of Medinipur Division. In the past, the Hindu god Krishna was worshiped as Keshto Ray in this temple.

Radha Shyam Temple, also known as Rādhāśyāma Mandir, is a Krishna temple in Bishnupur town of Medinipur division in the Indian state of West Bengal. In this temple, the Hindu god Krishna is worshiped in the form of Shyam; along with Shyam, the murti of Radha is also worshipped.

The Temples of Bishnupur are a group of temples located in the town of Bishnupur, West Bengal, India. These temples, built by the Malla kings between the 17th and 18th centuries, are renowned for their unique architectural style that blends traditional Bengali design with influences from other regions.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Rasmancha?
faq.a: The best time to visit Rasmancha is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata (124 km) and the nearest railway station is Howrah Junction (Kolkata) (118 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Rasmancha?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Rasmancha at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Rasmancha?
faq.a: Stays in Rasmancha start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Rasmancha?
faq.a: The top attractions are Shyam Ray Temple, Nikhil Banga Sikhsan Mahavidyalaya, Jor Bangla Temple, Mrinmoyee Temple, Radha Shyam Temple.
